# RUNBOOK — PDPC

<!-- Generated by `zeeker runbook` (zeeker 0.9.0) on 2026-07-16. -->

> **Regeneration policy:** this file is generated ONCE and then hand-maintained.
> Re-running `zeeker runbook --force` overwrites the ENTIRE file — including
> everything written into the TODO sections below. Nothing is preserved on
> regeneration. To refresh the auto-generated facts without losing hand-written
> content, generate a fresh copy elsewhere (`zeeker runbook --output RUNBOOK.new.md`)
> and merge manually.

This runbook is the operational document for humans and AI monitoring agents:
how to run builds, what healthy output looks like, and how to interpret
failures and skips.

## Project facts (auto-generated)

- **Project:** `PDPC`
- **Database file:** `pdpc.db` (Datasette database name: `pdpc`)
- **Title:** PDPC Enforcement Decisions
- **Description:** Non-commercial catalogue of the enforcement decisions of Singapore's Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC) under the Personal Data Protection Act 2012: Commission's Decisions and Voluntary Undertakings, with listing metadata, the summary blurb from each decision page, and a link to each decision document. The full decision text is fetched for indexing only; access to it is not provided through this catalogue — read each decision at its decision_url or pdf_url. Decision metadata and summaries are reproduced from the PDPC website (https://www.pdpc.gov.sg) and are the copyright of the Personal Data Protection Commission, Singapore; this use is licensed under the Terms and Conditions of Use of the PDPC Website (https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/terms-and-conditions) for non-commercial, informational and educational purposes. This is an independent project: it is not connected with, sponsored or endorsed by PDPC, and is not developed or published on behalf of, in collaboration with, or in partnership with PDPC. Fields such as organisation and penalty_amount are heuristic extractions from the published text, not authoritative — always check https://www.pdpc.gov.sg for the authoritative and latest version of each decision. The dataset is updated when the sync pipeline runs (currently triggered manually, not on a fixed schedule); each row's imported_on field records when it was captured, and decisions published since the last run will be absent.
- **Source:** Personal Data Protection Commission (https://www.pdpc.gov.sg)
- **Source URL:** https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/organisations/regulations-decisions/enforcement-decisions
- **License:** © Personal Data Protection Commission, Singapore. Contents reproduced from the PDPC website (www.pdpc.gov.sg); this use is licensed under the Terms and Conditions of Use of the PDPC Website (non-commercial, informational and educational use with attribution).
- **License URL:** https://www.pdpc.gov.sg/terms-and-conditions

### Resources

| Resource | Description | Fragments | Fragments on skip | FTS fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enforcement_decisions` | One row per PDPC enforcement decision — Commission's Decisions and Voluntary Undertakings (384 items on the listing as of June 2026). Listing metadata comes from PDPC's listing API; publication date, summary blurb, document link and penalty figure are parsed from each decision's detail page. Rows are insert-only: a decision is captured once, at discovery, and not re-fetched. organisation and penalty_amount are heuristic extractions — verify against the linked decision_url. Full decision text is in enforcement_decisions_fragments. | yes | no | `title`, `organisation`, `decision_type`, `summary` |
| `enforcement_decisions_fragments` | Full text of each decision document, chunked for search: the decision PDF is converted to markdown by a docling server and split into 1,200-character chunks with 150-character overlap. Populated by a backfill that runs inside every enforcement_decisions build (a small batch per run, so coverage grows over time); decisions whose PDF fetch or conversion fails repeatedly are quarantined and skipped. The converted markdown is approximate — the published PDF linked from pdf_url is the authoritative text. | no | no | — |
| `guidance_by_topic` | PDPC guidance publications, templates, tools, and training courses for organisations complying with the PDPA. | yes | no | `title`, `topic`, `summary` |
| `regulatory_guidance` | PDPC advisory guidelines, practical guidance, sector-specific guidelines, and industry-led guidelines interpreting the PDPA. | yes | no | `title`, `topic`, `summary` |

**`enforcement_decisions`**

- facets: `['decision_type']`
- size: `20`
- fragments fts fields: `['text']`
- label column: `title`
- fragments table: `enforcement_decisions_fragments`

**`enforcement_decisions_fragments`**

- sort: `sequence`

**`guidance_by_topic`**

- facets: `['topic']`
- sort: `published_date desc`
- size: `25`
- fragments fts fields: `['text']`
- label column: `title`
- fragments table: `guidance_by_topic_fragments`

**`regulatory_guidance`**

- facets: `['topic']`
- sort: `published_date desc`
- size: `25`
- fragments fts fields: `['text']`
- label column: `title`
- fragments table: `regulatory_guidance_fragments`

## Command reference (auto-generated)

```bash
uv run zeeker build                        # build all resources
uv run zeeker build <resource> [...]       # selective build
uv run zeeker build --sync-from-s3         # incremental: download existing DB from S3 first
uv run zeeker build --setup-fts            # (re)create FTS indexes on configured fields (idempotent)
uv run zeeker build --json                 # machine-readable BuildReport JSON on stdout
uv run zeeker build --progress-file p.json # atomic JSON snapshot after each resource (for watchers)
uv run zeeker build --fail-on-blocked      # exit 1 if any resource skipped with kind "blocked"
uv run zeeker deploy                       # upload the built .db to S3
```

## Status contract (auto-generated — zeeker 0.9.0)

This section is baked in verbatim from the installed zeeker version. Monitors
should parse builds against this contract, not against ad-hoc log scraping.

### Exit codes (`zeeker build`)

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `0` | All resources succeeded (skips are not failures). |
| `1` | One or more resources failed, FTS setup failed, post-hook exited non-zero, `--fail-on-empty` with a skipped resource, or `--fail-on-blocked` with a resource that skipped with kind `blocked`. |
| `2` | Fatal error: schema conflict, DB open failure, config error, local-diverged sync. No coherent build ran. |

### Non-TTY line grammar (plain text output, what agents see)

One status line per resource, streamed as each finishes:

```
[OK  ] <resource>  <N> records  (<T>s)
[FAIL] <resource>  <error message>  (<T>s)
[SKIP] <resource>  <reason> (<kind>)  (<T>s)
[SKIP] <resource>  no data returned  (<T>s)
WARN[<resource>]: <message>
WARN[build]: <message>
SUMMARY: <S> succeeded, <F> failed, <K> skipped in <T>s | <k1>=<v1> <k2>=<v2>
FTS_ERROR: <message>
POST_HOOK: <command> exit=<code>
FATAL: <message>
```

- `[SKIP] name  <reason> (kind)` — the resource raised `Skip(reason, kind=...)`;
  the reason says WHY. `[SKIP] name  no data returned` is a plain `return []`
  skip (implicit kind `up_to_date`, no reason).
- Enrichment counters (see below) are appended to `[OK]`/`[SKIP]` lines as
  `; key=value key=value` (never to `[FAIL]` lines), e.g.
  `[SKIP] judgments  no data returned; updated=50 enriched=25  (12.3s)`.
- `WARN[<resource>]:` lines follow that resource's status line; `WARN[build]:`
  lines are build-level warnings (e.g. S3 sync issues).
- The `SUMMARY:` footer aggregates `extra_counts` across all resources after
  a ` | ` separator (omitted when there are no counters).

### Skip kinds and what each means for a monitor

| Kind | Meaning | Bumps `_zeeker_updates.last_updated`? | Monitor action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `up_to_date` | Source was checked; nothing new. The healthy steady state. | Yes | None. |
| `blocked` | A precondition failed (proxy down, missing env var) — the source was NOT checked. | No (time-based incremental resources stay safe) | Actionable: investigate the precondition. Use `--fail-on-blocked` to turn this into exit 1. |
| `disabled` | Intentionally switched off (feature flag / config). | No | None — not an incident. |

### Enrichment counters (`__zeeker_report__`)

Multi-phase resources that UPDATE existing rows (0 records inserted) report
work via a module-level dict in the resource module:

```python
def fetch_data(existing_table):
    global __zeeker_report__
    __zeeker_report__ = {"updated": 50, "enriched": 25, "notes": "phase2 drained 25"}
    return []
```

- Read and cleared on EVERY exit path — success, skip, even a fetch_data crash
  (work done before the crash stays visible).
- Re-read after the fragments phase; counters set in `fetch_fragments_data`
  merge in, summing on key collision.
- Int values only (other value types are ignored, never fail the build).
- Rendered as `key=value` on status lines, aggregated totals in the `SUMMARY:`
  footer, and carried as `extra_counts` / `notes` in `--json`.
- A "0 succeeded" SUMMARY with non-zero counters means real enrichment work
  happened — do not read it as a dead build.

### JSON payload (`--json` and `--progress-file` share the same schema)

Top-level fields:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `status` | string | `success` \| `partial_failure` \| `failed` \| `fatal` |
| `total_duration_s` | float | Whole-build wall time. |
| `resources` | array | One object per resource (fields below). |
| `build_warnings` | array of strings | Build-level warnings (`WARN[build]:` lines). |
| `fts_error` | string or null | FTS setup failure, if any. |
| `fatal_error` | string or null | Set when the build died before/without a per-resource report. |
| `post_hook` | object or null | `{command, exit_code, stdout, stderr}` when `--post-hook` ran. |

Per-resource object fields:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | Resource name. |
| `status` | string | `success` \| `failed` \| `skipped` |
| `records` | int | New main-table rows inserted. |
| `duration_s` | float | Per-resource wall time. |
| `error_message` | string or null | Set on failure. |
| `traceback` | string or null | Full Python traceback on failure (paths CWD-relativized). |
| `fragments_records` | int or null | Fragment rows inserted, when a fragments phase ran. |
| `skip_reason` | string or null | The `Skip(...)` reason; null for plain returned-`[]` skips. |
| `skip_kind` | string or null | `up_to_date` \| `blocked` \| `disabled` (only meaningful when skipped). |
| `warnings` | array of strings | Per-resource warnings (`WARN[<resource>]:` lines). |
| `extra_counts` | object | Int counters from `__zeeker_report__`. |
| `notes` | string or null | Free-text note from `__zeeker_report__`. |

---

## What happens during a run

All three data resources (`enforcement_decisions`, `guidance_by_topic`,
`regulatory_guidance`) are near-clones of the same pipeline. Every request to
pdpc.gov.sg goes through the Tailscale SOCKS5 proxy (`TAILSCALE_PROXY`) because
PDPC's CloudFront blocks datacenter IPs; PDF text extraction goes through a
Docling server (`DOCLING_SERVE_URL`). Under zeeker 0.9.0 each resource's
`fetch_data()` runs exactly ONCE per build (the module is loaded once; there is
no re-fetch between the main and fragments phases).

Per-resource phase order inside that single `fetch_data()` call:

1. **Dedupe** (`enforcement_decisions` only): removes existing rows that share a
   `decision_url` (PDPC republishes decisions under new listing UUIDs), keeping
   the earliest-imported row and dropping orphaned fragments. Reported as
   `dedupe_removed` when nonzero.
2. **Fragment backfill** (side effect, runs even on 0-new builds): picks up to
   `PDPC_*_BACKFILL_BATCH_SIZE` (default 10) rows that have a `pdf_url` but no
   fragments yet, fetches each PDF through the proxy, converts it via Docling,
   and inserts 1,200-char chunks (150 overlap) directly into the fragments
   table. Failures are tracked per item in a JSON checkpoint; items at the
   retry cap are quarantined with a TTL (see "Failure modes").
3. **Discovery**: pages through PDPC's listing API (`/api/listing-api`,
   newest-first, 10/page). Stops early once a whole page of items is already
   known. For each new item it fetches the detail page (Next.js RSC stream),
   parses date/summary/PDF link, then fetches + Docling-converts the PDF
   inline. If even listing page 1 cannot be fetched, the resource emits
   `ABORTED (listing fetch failed: <TypeName>: <msg>)` on stderr and raises
   `Skip(..., kind="blocked")` — the freshness marker is NOT advanced.

After `fetch_data()` returns new rows, zeeker runs:

4. **transform_data()**: strips the internal `_pdf_text` field so the stored
   rows never carry the full document text (licensing: full text lives only in
   the fragments tables).
5. **Fragments phase** (only when new rows were inserted):
   `fetch_fragments_data()` receives the pre-transform rows as
   `main_data_context` and chunks each row's `_pdf_text` into
   `{uuid}_chunk_{seq}` fragments. Rows whose PDF failed to extract this build
   have empty `_pdf_text` and are left for the backfill (step 2) on later runs.

`enforcement_decisions_fragments` is a stub resource (always
`[SKIP] no data returned`) — it exists only so zeeker.toml can carry the
fragments table's column descriptions.

**Typical healthy steady-state log** (0 new publications, backfill working):

```
enforcement_decisions: Fragment backfill: 14 pending (2 re-eligible after quarantine TTL), 114 quarantined total — processing 10 this run.
  enforcement_decisions: OK  a1b2c3d4 | Breach of the Protection Obligation by ... | 12 chunks
enforcement_decisions: Fragment backfill done: 9 OK, 1 failed, 5 still pending, 114 quarantined total (+0 this run).
enforcement_decisions: Existing records: 371
enforcement_decisions: Total decisions: 384 across 39 pages
  enforcement_decisions: All items on page 1 already known — stopping.

enforcement_decisions: Done: 0 new enforcement decisions.
[SKIP] enforcement_decisions  no data returned; fragments_ok=9 fragments_failed=1 still_pending=5 quarantined_total=114  (312.4s)
SUMMARY: 0 succeeded, 0 failed, 4 skipped in 640.2s | fragments_ok=27 fragments_failed=3 ...
```

A "0 succeeded" SUMMARY with nonzero `fragments_ok` is a healthy build — the
backfill did real work even though no new rows were discovered.

## Environment variables

Secrets live in GitHub Actions secrets (`Settings > Secrets and variables >
Actions`); local runs can use a `.env` file (auto-loaded by zeeker).

**Required for a useful build:**

| Var | Used for | When unset |
|---|---|---|
| `TAILSCALE_PROXY` | SOCKS5 proxy for ALL pdpc.gov.sg requests (e.g. `socks5h://172.17.0.1:1055`). CloudFront blocks datacenter IPs. | Every listing fetch 403s/times out → all three resources skip with kind `blocked`; backfill fails every item in its batch. |
| `DOCLING_SERVE_URL` | PDF → markdown conversion (`POST /v1/convert/file`) | Defaults to `http://localhost:5001`; if unreachable, PDF extraction fails — new rows are still inserted (with empty fragments, left for backfill), backfill batch items all fail and accrue checkpoint failures. |

**Required for deploy only:** `S3_BUCKET`, `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`,
`AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `S3_ENDPOINT_URL` (non-AWS S3). Also used by
`zeeker build --sync-from-s3` and `zeeker backup`.

**Optional backfill knobs (shared quarantine logic in `resources/_backfill.py`):**

| Var | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `PDPC_BACKFILL_RETRY_AFTER` | `1209600` (14 days) | Quarantine TTL in seconds — quarantined items become eligible again once `last_attempt` is older than this. |
| `PDPC_BACKFILL_RETRY_QUARANTINED` | unset | Set `1` for a one-run manual flush: ALL quarantined items become eligible regardless of TTL. |
| `PDPC_BACKFILL_BATCH_SIZE` / `PDPC_BACKFILL_MAX_RETRIES` | `10` / `3` | enforcement_decisions batch size and quarantine cap. |
| `PDPC_GUIDANCE_BACKFILL_BATCH_SIZE` / `PDPC_GUIDANCE_BACKFILL_MAX_RETRIES` | `10` / `3` | guidance_by_topic knobs. |
| `PDPC_REGGUIDANCE_BACKFILL_BATCH_SIZE` / `PDPC_REGGUIDANCE_BACKFILL_MAX_RETRIES` | `10` / `3` | regulatory_guidance knobs. |

## Cadence & expected yield

**Schedule:** weekly, Mondays 03:00 UTC (11:00 SGT), via two workflows:

- `.github/workflows/sync-enforcement-decisions.yml` — `enforcement_decisions`
- `.github/workflows/sync-pdpc-guidance.yml` — `guidance_by_topic` + `regulatory_guidance`

Both run `zeeker build --sync-from-s3 <resource> --setup-fts` (the flag is
idempotent as of zeeker 0.9.0 and rebuilds the FTS index on every run), then
`zeeker deploy` and `zeeker backup`.

**Normal yield (PDPC publishes infrequently — 0 new is the norm):**

| Resource | New rows/week | DB size (Jul 2026) |
|---|---|---|
| `enforcement_decisions` | 0–2 | ~371 rows |
| `guidance_by_topic` | 0 most weeks | ~79 rows |
| `regulatory_guidance` | 0–2 | ~42 rows |

**Duration:** 5–45 minutes per workflow, dominated by PDF fetches through the
proxy plus Docling conversion (roughly 30–120 s per document in the backfill
batch). A listing-only steady-state pass with an empty backfill queue finishes
in seconds.

**Anomalies worth flagging:**

- Any `[SKIP] ... (blocked)` — the proxy (or the listing API) is down; the
  source was never checked and the freshness marker did not advance.
- `fragments_failed` equal to the whole batch on several consecutive builds —
  Docling or the proxy is broken for PDFs specifically.
- `quarantined_total` climbing steadily — new items are failing through to
  quarantine faster than the TTL re-tries them.
- Workflow duration near the 90-minute timeout.
- `dedupe_removed` > 0 more than occasionally (expected only when PDPC
  republishes a batch of Voluntary Undertakings).

## Failure modes & recovery

**Proxy down (most common).** Log shows `Routing via socks5h://...` followed by
`ABORTED (listing fetch failed: RetryError: ...ProxyError...)` on stderr, and
the resource skips with kind `blocked`. Duration can be long (tenacity retries
with backoff: 3 attempts per HTTP call). Recovery: bring the Tailscale exit
node back up and re-run the workflow (`workflow_dispatch`). Because a blocked
skip does not advance `_zeeker_updates.last_updated`, nothing published during
the outage is lost — the next successful discovery pass picks it up.

**Listing OK, some pages fail.** `Listing page N failed: <TypeName>: ...`
(stderr); a circuit breaker stops discovery after 5 consecutive page failures
(`Too many failures — stopping.`). Already-collected new rows are still
inserted; the missed pages are re-walked next build.

**PDF extraction failure at discovery time.** `PDF extract failed for <uuid>`
or `PDF extract too short for <uuid> (N chars < 50) — leaving for backfill`
(stderr). The main row is inserted with an empty fragment set; the backfill
retries the PDF on later builds. Not an incident by itself.

**Backfill failures & quarantine.** Per-item failure state lives in JSON
checkpoints (gitignored, kept warm on the runner only via the workflow
artifacts):

- `checkpoint_pdpc_fragments.json` (enforcement_decisions)
- `checkpoint_guidance_fragments.json` (guidance_by_topic)
- `checkpoint_regulatory_guidance_fragments.json` (regulatory_guidance)

Shape: `{item_id: {failures, last_error, last_attempt}}`. An item reaching the
retry cap (default 3) is quarantined — but only for a TTL
(`PDPC_BACKFILL_RETRY_AFTER`, default 14 days ≈ two weekly builds), after
which it is retried once; a re-failure re-quarantines it for another window.
The failure count is preserved across quarantine cycles. Manual overrides:

- `PDPC_BACKFILL_RETRY_QUARANTINED=1` — flush the whole quarantine for one run.
- Delete a checkpoint file — resets all failure counts for that resource
  (safe: it only tracks failures; completed fragments live in the DB).
- Missing/malformed `last_attempt` fails open (item is retried), so corrupt
  checkpoint data can never strand an item.

**Docling down.** Every backfill item fails with `ConnectError`/`ReadTimeout`;
`fragments_failed` equals the batch size. Items accrue failures toward
quarantine — if the outage lasts multiple builds, expect a `quarantined_total`
bump, then TTL-driven recovery once Docling is back. For a long planned outage,
consider deleting the checkpoints afterwards to fast-track retries.

**Duplicate rows (decision_url republished).** Self-healing:
`_dedupe_existing_decisions` runs on every enforcement_decisions build and
removes them (`dedupe_removed` counter).

**Schema conflict noise.** The schema sample zeeker checks is the PRE-transform
fetch output, which carries the internal `_pdf_text` field; each resource's
`migrate_schema()` drops it from the inferred schema (and pins
`penalty_amount` to REAL on enforcement_decisions), so an occasional
"Schema migration successful" info line on builds with new rows is expected,
not a problem.

**What is safe to delete:** `pdpc.db` (recoverable via `--sync-from-s3` or a
full re-crawl), any `checkpoint_*.json` (resets failure tracking only). There
are no other on-disk caches — PDF bytes are never persisted locally.

## Backlog / progress queries

Run with `sqlite3 pdpc.db` (or `uv run sqlite-utils query pdpc.db "..."`).

**Fragment-backfill backlog** — rows that have a document but no fragments yet
(the population the backfill draws from; the checkpoint only tracks failures):

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enforcement_decisions d
WHERE d.pdf_url != '' AND d.pdf_url IS NOT NULL
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM enforcement_decisions_fragments f WHERE f.parent_id = d.id);

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM guidance_by_topic g
WHERE g.pdf_url != '' AND g.pdf_url IS NOT NULL
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM guidance_by_topic_fragments f WHERE f.parent_id = g.id);

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM regulatory_guidance r
WHERE r.pdf_url != '' AND r.pdf_url IS NOT NULL
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM regulatory_guidance_fragments f WHERE f.parent_id = r.id);
```

A healthy pipeline shrinks these numbers by roughly `fragments_ok` per build
(minus any newly discovered rows). Cross-check against the `still_pending` and
`quarantined_total` counters on the build status line: backlog ≈ still_pending
+ quarantined (quarantined items are excluded from the eligible set until
their TTL expires).

**Duplicate check (should always return 0):**

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) - COUNT(DISTINCT decision_url) FROM enforcement_decisions
WHERE decision_url IS NOT NULL AND decision_url != '';
```

**Orphaned fragments (should be 0):**

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM enforcement_decisions_fragments f
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM enforcement_decisions d WHERE d.id = f.parent_id);
```

**Freshness** — when was each resource last actually checked (blocked/disabled
skips do NOT advance this):

```sql
SELECT resource_name, last_updated, record_count FROM _zeeker_updates;
```

**Quarantine inspection** (filesystem, not SQL):

```bash
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('checkpoint_pdpc_fragments.json'));
print(len([v for v in d.values() if v['failures']>=3]), 'quarantined');
[print(k[:8], v['failures'], v['last_error'][:60]) for k,v in sorted(d.items(), key=lambda kv:-kv[1]['failures'])[:10]]"
```

## Escalation

There is no automated paging for this project; GitHub Actions email/UI is the
alert surface. Guidance for a monitoring agent:

- **One `blocked` skip:** note it, no escalation — the weekly cadence tolerates
  a missed run (nothing is lost thanks to the freshness-marker rule).
- **Two consecutive weekly runs blocked:** escalate to the repo owner — the
  Tailscale exit node (ASUS router) or the Docling host likely needs a human.
- **Exit code 1/2 (real failure, not a skip):** escalate immediately with the
  `traceback` from `--json`; this usually means a source-format change (RSC
  stream layout, listing API) that needs a code fix.
- **Deploy step failure:** the built DB is still uploaded as a workflow
  artifact (7-day retention) — recover it from there rather than re-crawling.
- Useful context for whoever picks it up: this RUNBOOK, `CLAUDE.md`
  (development guide), and the per-run workflow artifacts
  (`pdpc.db`, `metadata.json`, checkpoint files).
